problems.name: Severe acute biliary pancreatitis (resolved)
problems.diagnosedBy: Dr. Marcus Hong / Dr. Jennifer Beckman (admission 2/15/2027)
problems.notes: Clinically and biochemically resolved. Status post emergent ERCP 2/16/2027 with sphincterotomy + CBD stone extraction; biliary E. coli identified. Source-controlled and treated with 13 days of pip/tazo.

problems.name: Right lower extremity proximal deep vein thrombosis (acute, provoked)
problems.diagnosedBy: Dr. Allyson Pishko / Dr. Maya Singh (Hematology consult)
problems.notes: Right CFV through popliteal vein. Provoked by prolonged ICU course + immobility + severe pancreatitis. On apixaban 5 mg PO BID since 3/9/2027 (after 7 days at 10 mg BID loading). Planned ≥3-month treatment duration; outpatient hematology to reassess.

problems.name: Cholelithiasis (chronic, symptomatic)
problems.diagnosedBy: Dr. Sandra Kim (Hopkins Bayview UC, US 9/30/2025)
problems.notes: Known multiple gallstones in distended gallbladder. Prior surgery deferred by patient. Now symptomatic with severe acute biliary pancreatitis (2/2027) — elective cholecystectomy planned outpatient ~6/2027 with General Surgery (Dr. Markovich).

problems.name: Hypertension (essential)
problems.notes: Well-controlled chronic HTN, currently on lisinopril 5 mg PO daily (dose decreased from 10 to 5 in 1/2026 with CKD progression). BP at goal.

problems.name: Hyperlipidemia
problems.notes: Initial LDL 168. Well-controlled on atorvastatin 40 mg daily; LDL at goal <100.

problems.name: Chronic kidney disease, stage 3a
problems.diagnosedBy: Dr. Phong Le (incidentally noted on annual labs)
problems.notes: Baseline Cr 1.3 (eGFR 47). Slow progression from 0.9 over 12 years. Contributors: HTN, chronic NSAID use (now minimized), age. AKI superimposed during 2/2027 admission resolved to baseline. AVOID NSAIDs and IV contrast (pre-medicate if essential).

problems.name: Latent tuberculosis infection (untreated)
problems.notes: PPD 14 mm + IGRA positive in 2010 (workplace screening). 8 documented declinations of INH treatment over 16 years. Active TB ruled out during 2/2027 admission (3 AFB smears + Xpert MTB/RIF negative). Continue monitoring for symptoms of reactivation.

problems.name: Right TKA (status post 4/2018)
problems.diagnosedBy: Dr. Marcus Reilly (Orthopedic Surgery, Hopkins Bayview)
problems.notes: Excellent outcome; no acute issues. Surgical history reference only.

problems.name: Left knee osteoarthritis
problems.notes: KL grade 2. Conservative management with topical diclofenac gel + acetaminophen. AVOID systemic NSAIDs given CKD + apixaban.

problems.name: Anemia, chronic (CKD-related, anemia of chronic disease)
problems.notes: Hgb chronically 11.5-12.0 baseline. Nadir Hgb 7.4 during ICU; received 1 unit PRBC. Discharge Hgb 10.5 — improving toward baseline. No active bleeding.

problems.name: Osteopenia
problems.notes: T-score -1.8 femoral neck on 2020 DEXA. On calcium + vitamin D supplementation. No fragility fractures.

problems.name: Pre-diabetes
problems.notes: HbA1c 5.6-5.8 historically. Diet/lifestyle counseling only. Will reassess at outpatient follow-up.

problems.name: Right upper lobe pulmonary nodule, 2.5 cm
problems.diagnosedBy: Hopkins Radiology (Dr. Sandra Wu CXR 2/16; Dr. Anil Vachani CT 3/15)
problems.notes: INCIDENTAL FINDING during sepsis workup admission. Solitary 2.5 cm well-circumscribed solid lesion in apical segment of RUL. Stable across 4 weeks of serial imaging (CXRs 2/16, 2/17, 2/18; dedicated CT 3/15). Active TB ruled out this admission. **FOLLOW-UP TO BE OPERATIONALIZED — currently no specific outpatient appointment booked for this finding.** Note: this problem was added to the active problem list on 4/12/2027 (discharge day) by the medicine team upon final review of imaging.

problems.name: Penicillin allergy
problems.notes: Childhood rash, non-anaphylactic, never re-challenged. Tolerated piperacillin-tazobactam during 2/2027 admission without reaction — confirms low cross-reactivity for current β-lactams in the family.

problems.name: Iodinated contrast allergy
problems.notes: Urticarial reaction with facial flushing during 2019 contrast CT. Subsequent contrast studies (including 2/2027 admission CT and 3/15/2027 follow-up chest CT) have used Hopkins Radiology pre-medication protocol (prednisone at 13, 7, 1 hr + diphenhydramine at 1 hr) without reaction. PRE-MEDICATION REQUIRED FOR ANY FUTURE IV CONTRAST IMAGING.
